FAQs/CloudAssure/AssesmentandAdvisory.title: Assesment and Advisory
FAQs/CloudAssure/AssesmentandAdvisory.description: What are Cloud Assessment Services?Cloud Assessment and Advisory is a structured engagement that thoroughly evaluates an organization’s IT infrastructure, applications, and processes to determine cloud readiness. The outcome is a tailored roadmap for migration and modernization aligned with business objectives, operational needs, and future scalability.

FAQs/CloudAssure/CloudAssure.title: Cloud Assure
FAQs/CloudAssure/CloudAssure.description: What is Cloud Assure Service (CAS)?Cloud Assure is a comprehensive cloud management service suite designed to help enterprises manage, secure, and optimize their cloud environments across public (Yntraa Cloud, AWS, Azure), private, hybrid, and multi-cloud setups. It covers the full lifecycle from assessment and migration to operations, security, optimization, and professional services.

FAQs/CloudAssure/MigrationAssist.title: Migration Assist
FAQs/CloudAssure/MigrationAssist.description: What is Cloud Migration Assist?Cloud Migration Assist is a structured service that supports all types of migrations, including on-premises to cloud, cloud-to-cloud, and legacy environment migrations. It ensures secure and efficient movement of workloads, applications, databases, and data with minimal disruption and a controlled transition.

FAQs/CloudAssure/ProfessionalService.title: Professional Service
FAQs/CloudAssure/ProfessionalService.description: What is Cloud Professional Service?Cloud Professional Service is a specialized, project-based consulting and implementation offering under the Cloud Assure portfolio. It helps organizations maximize cloud adoption, transformation, and innovation through tailored engagements spanning the entire cloud lifecycle—from strategy and adoption to optimization and continuous improvement.

FAQs/CloudAssure/ResiliencyAssuranceServ.title: Resiliency Assurance Service
FAQs/CloudAssure/ResiliencyAssuranceServ.description: What is a Disaster?Disaster is a natural or man-made (or technological) hazard resulting in an event of substantial extent causing significant physical damage or destruction or loss.

FAQs/ManagedCompute/VirtualDesktop.title: Virtual Desktop
FAQs/ManagedCompute/VirtualDesktop.description: What is Virtual Desktop-as-a-Service (vDaaS)?vDaaS is a cloud-based service that delivers secure, fully functional Windows or Linux desktops to users over the internet, supporting flexible, remote, and hybrid work across all devices. Unlike traditional PCs, these desktops are hosted in secure data centers, eliminating on-site hardware and enabling quick, scalable desktop infrastructure management.

FAQs/ManagedStorage/File Storage.title: File Storage
FAQs/ManagedStorage/File Storage.description: What is File Storage in STaaS?File Storage offers shared access using standard file protocols (NFS/SMB), suitable for collaborative workspaces, media editing, and centralized storage environments.

FAQs/Miscellaneous/FAQs.title: Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
FAQs/Miscellaneous/FAQs.description: What happens if I miss a payment due date?Depending on how your service provider has set up the payment collections policies, one of the following might happen:

FAQs/OtherServices/ManagedDatabases.title: Managed Databases
FAQs/OtherServices/ManagedDatabases.description: What is Yntraa Cloud Managed Database as a Service (MDBaaS)?Yntraa Cloud MDBaaS is a fully managed database solution that offers enterprises scalable, secure, and high-performance databases without the need to manage the underlying infrastructure.

Knowledgebase/IPv4Networking.title: What is IPv4 Networking?
Knowledgebase/IPv4Networking.description: Internet Protocol version 4(IPv4) is the fourth generation of the Internet Protocol, and it remains the most commonly used system for assigning addresses to devices connected to a network. An IPv4 address is made up of four numbers separated by dots—like 10.0.1.15—and each address is unique, ensuring that data sent over the internet reaches the right device.

Knowledgebase/WhatisaVirtualFirewallinth.title: What is a Virtual Firewall in the Cloud?
Knowledgebase/WhatisaVirtualFirewallinth.description: A virtual firewall is a software-based tool used to protect cloud systems. Just like a physical firewall protects a building from fire, a virtual firewall helps protect your cloud network from harmful or unwanted traffic. It controls the flow of data between different parts of your system and blocks anything suspicious or risky. This keeps your applications and data safe, whether traffic is coming from outside the cloud or moving between parts of the same system.

Knowledgebase/WhatisaVPC.title: What is a VPC?
Knowledgebase/WhatisaVPC.description: A Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) is a secure, isolated section of a public cloud where you can deploy and manage your cloud resources—such as virtual machines, databases, and applications—with full control over networking and security. With a VPC, you define your own IP address ranges, create subnets, configure routing tables, and set up firewalls and gateways, allowing you to build a custom network architecture tailored to your needs.

Knowledgebase/WhatisInstances.title: What is Instances?
Knowledgebase/WhatisInstances.description: Cloud computing allows users to run virtual machines called instances, which are flexible, on-demand servers that can run different operating systems. The two most common types of instances available in cloud environments are Linux instances and Windows instances. Both serve unique purposes and cater to different application needs.

Knowledgebase/WhatisIPAddressSubnetTiera.title: What is IP Address, Subnet, Tier and CIDR?
Knowledgebase/WhatisIPAddressSubnetTiera.description: Every device or service in a network—whether it's a virtual machine, load balancer, or database—needs a unique IP address to talk to other devices. It’s like a digital address for communication. For example, a VM may be assigned 192.168.1.10, which allows it to send and receive data across the network.

ReleaseNotes/Ver1000.title: Version 1.0.0.0
ReleaseNotes/Ver1000.description: We are excited to announce the general availability of Yntraa Cloud Platform, a modern cloud infrastructure platform designed to support enterprise applications, data platforms, and digital services.

Subscribers/AccountCentre/StandardAgreem.title: Standard Agreements
Subscribers/AccountCentre/StandardAgreem.description: Standard agreements and documents are available on the Yntraa Cloud Account Centre. Whenever any of these documents is amended or updated, you will be prompted to accept the modified documents on your next login.

Subscribers/AccountCentre/Subscriptionsa.title: Subscriptions and Services
Subscribers/AccountCentre/Subscriptionsa.description: A subscription is a record created whenever a new resource is purchased or a service is activated. In other words, a subscription is a financial ‘agreement’ that links the item that has been purchased, its value, and its renewal frequency.

Subscribers/AccountCentre/TeamMemberMana.title: Team Member Management
Subscribers/AccountCentre/TeamMemberMana.description: Team members can be onboarded using the Team section from the Account Centre, allows you to add commercial, technology or other admin users who can log in to your account and perform operations.

Subscribers/CloudAssure/AssessmentandAdv.title: Assessment and Advisory
Subscribers/CloudAssure/AssessmentandAdv.description: Cloud Assessment and Advisory Service helps organizations assess their current IT landscape and define a clear, strategic roadmap for cloud adoption. It provides expert guidance and tailored recommendations to ensure a secure, cost-effective, and well-aligned cloud transformation.

Subscribers/CloudAssure/MigrationAssist.title: Migration Assist
Subscribers/CloudAssure/MigrationAssist.description: Cloud Migration Service enables the secure and seamless transition of applications, data, and infrastructure to cloud or hybrid environments. Through a structured approach and tailored migration strategies, it minimizes risk and disruption while accelerating the benefits of cloud adoption.

Subscribers/CloudAssure/MonitoringandNot.title: Monitoring and Notifications
Subscribers/CloudAssure/MonitoringandNot.description: Cloud Monitoring and Notification Service ensures continuous visibility into the health and performance of cloud resources through real-time monitoring, centralized logging, and automated alerts. It enables timely issue detection and response, helping maintain uptime,

Subscribers/CloudAssure/ProfessionalServ.title: Professional Services
Subscribers/CloudAssure/ProfessionalServ.description: Cloud Professional Services provide structured, expert-led support to help organizations plan, implement, and optimize their cloud environments. These services cover the complete cloud lifecycle, including strategy, migration, modernization, and ongoing improvement.

Subscribers/Compute/ManagedKubernetes/Ab.title: About Kubernetes
Subscribers/Compute/ManagedKubernetes/Ab.description: Yntraa Cloud users the ability to deploy highly-available Kubernetes clusters for their cloud-native development, deployment and DevOps use cases. The Yntraa Kubernetes Service extends basic and advanced cluster management functionalities via the cloud console UI and the kubectl utility.

Subscribers/Compute/ManagedKubernetes/Ab.title: About Kubernetes Dashboard
Subscribers/Compute/ManagedKubernetes/Ab.description: The Kubernetes Dashboard is a web-based user interface that provides a visual representation of a cluster's resources and enables easier management and monitoring. Here's an overview of the dashboard:

Subscribers/Compute/ManagedKubernetes/Ac.title: Accessing a Cluster using the Command Line
Subscribers/Compute/ManagedKubernetes/Ac.description: Kubernetes clusters can be accessed and controlled from the command line using the kubeconfig manifest for the cluster. Each cluster has a unique manifest which is required to identify and target the cluster using the kubectl utility.

Subscribers/Compute/ManagedKubernetes/Ac.title: Accessing the Kubernetes Dashboard
Subscribers/Compute/ManagedKubernetes/Ac.description: To access the dashboard in Kubernetes version 1.24 and onwards, the following steps are:

Subscribers/Compute/ManagedKubernetes/Cl.title: Cluster Operations
Subscribers/Compute/ManagedKubernetes/Cl.description: Kubernetes clusters created on Yntraa Cloud allow for a set of management operations from the cloud console UI. While these options can be controlled using kubectl, these are provided on the UI for quick and easy access.

Subscribers/Compute/ManagedKubernetes/Cr.title: Creating a Kubernetes Cluster
Subscribers/Compute/ManagedKubernetes/Cr.description: To create a Kubernetes cluster, the following steps are:

Subscribers/Compute/ManagedKubernetes/In.title: Ingress Networking on Kubernetes Clusters
Subscribers/Compute/ManagedKubernetes/In.description: To view networking, navigate to Kubernetes and select a cluster details. This section manage a cluster's firewalls and ingress networking. Here, Port forwarding and load balancing rules can also be managed using kubectl.

Subscribers/Compute/ManagedKubernetes/Ov.title: Overview
Subscribers/Compute/ManagedKubernetes/Ov.description: To view the below details, navigate to Kubernetes, select a Kubernetes Cluster, and access the Overview tab.

Subscribers/Compute/ManagedKubernetes/Sc.title: Scaling Kubernetes Clusters
Subscribers/Compute/ManagedKubernetes/Sc.description: Yntraa Cloud allows for manual and automatic cluster scaling. Clusters scaling can be configured from the Nodes section of cluster details or from the Operations section of cluster details.

Subscribers/Compute/ManagedKubernetes/Vi.title: Viewing Kubernetes Cluster Details
Subscribers/Compute/ManagedKubernetes/Vi.description: Yntraa Cloud offers a detailed view of Kubernetes clusters on the UI. Yntraa also brings the full power and accessibility of cluster management via the kubectl interface.

Subscribers/Compute/OtherLinuxInstances/.description: Yntraa Cloud offers a highly usable and visual way of working with and operating Linux Instances using the cloud console. You can access all the Linux Instances available in your account in the following way:

Subscribers/Compute/OtherLinuxInstances/.title: Connecting to a Linux Instance
Subscribers/Compute/OtherLinuxInstances/.description: You can connect to Linux Instances via SSH sessions using passwords (less secure) or SSH key pairs (most secure).

Subscribers/Compute/OtherLinuxInstances/.title: Creating Alerts
Subscribers/Compute/OtherLinuxInstances/.description: To view the configured alerts or configure new ones, navigate to the Operating Linux Instances, and select Linux Instance and access the Alerts tab.

Subscribers/Compute/OtherLinuxInstances/.title: Creating Linux Instances
Subscribers/Compute/OtherLinuxInstances/.description: Before creating a Linux instance, it is important to plan the architecture, networking and access to the Linux Instances.

Subscribers/Compute/OtherLinuxInstances/.title: Networking Management
Subscribers/Compute/OtherLinuxInstances/.description: To view the networks of particular Instance, navigate to Operating Linux Instances, select a Linux Instance and access the Networking tab.

Subscribers/Compute/OtherLinuxInstances/.description: To view all available Instance operations, navigate to the Operating Linux Instances, select a Linux Instance and access the Operations tab.

Subscribers/Compute/RHELInstances/AboutR.title: About RHEL Instances
Subscribers/Compute/RHELInstances/AboutR.description: Instances are computing units that provide you with resources to run your applications/workloads. Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) Instances are virtual machines that run the images of RHEL Operating System.

Subscribers/Compute/RHELInstances/Connec.title: Connecting to a RHEL Instance
Subscribers/Compute/RHELInstances/Connec.description: You can connect to RHEL Instances via SSH sessions using passwords (less secure) or SSH key pairs (most secure).

Subscribers/Compute/RHELInstances/Creati.title: Creating Alerts
Subscribers/Compute/RHELInstances/Creati.description: Alerts get triggered whenever a configured condition is met. You can create multiple alerts on an instance. Alerts are sent to recipients that you can define and manage.

Subscribers/Compute/RHELInstances/Creati.title: Creating RHEL Instances
Subscribers/Compute/RHELInstances/Creati.description: Before creating a RHEL instance, it is important to plan the architecture, networking and access to the RHEL Instances.

Subscribers/Compute/RHELInstances/Reconf.description: To view available reconfiguration options, navigate to RHEL Instances and access the Reconfigure tab.

Subscribers/Compute/RHELInstances/RHELIn.description: To view all available Instance operations, navigate to RHEL Instances and access the Operations tab.

Subscribers/Compute/RHELInstances/Viewin.title: Viewing Graphs and Utilisation
Subscribers/Compute/RHELInstances/Viewin.description: To view the available graphs and monitor this instance in real-time, navigate to RHEL Instance and access the Graphs tab.

Subscribers/Compute/RHELInstances/Viewin.title: Viewing RHEL Instances
Subscribers/Compute/RHELInstances/Viewin.description: Yntraa Cloud offers a highly usable and visual way of working with and operating RHEL Instances using the cloud console. All RHEL Instances available in your account can be accessed in the following way:

Subscribers/Compute/RHELInstances/Volume.description: To view the disks attached to particular Instance, navigate to RHEL Instances and access the Volumes tab.

Subscribers/Compute/WindowsInstances/Abo.title: About Windows Instances
Subscribers/Compute/WindowsInstances/Abo.description: Instances are computing units that provide you with resources to run your applications/workloads. Windows Instances are virtual machines that run the images of Windows Operating Systems.

Subscribers/Compute/WindowsInstances/Con.title: Connecting to a Windows Instance
Subscribers/Compute/WindowsInstances/Con.description: Unlike Linux Instances that use SSH connections, Windows Instances use the remote desktop protocol (RDP).

Subscribers/Compute/WindowsInstances/Cre.title: Creating Alerts on Windows Instances
Subscribers/Compute/WindowsInstances/Cre.description: To view the configured alerts or configure new ones, navigate to Operating Windows Instances, select a Windows Instance and access the Alerts tab .

Subscribers/Compute/WindowsInstances/Cre.title: Creating Windows Instances
Subscribers/Compute/WindowsInstances/Cre.description: Before creating a Windows Instance, it is important to plan the architecture, networking and access to the Windows Instances.

Subscribers/Compute/WindowsInstances/Net.title: Networking Management on Windows Instances
Subscribers/Compute/WindowsInstances/Net.description: To view the networks that a particular Instance is attached to, navigate to the Windows Instances Screen, select a Windows Instance, and access the Networking tab.

Subscribers/Compute/WindowsInstances/Rec.title: Reconfiguring Windows Instances
Subscribers/Compute/WindowsInstances/Rec.description: To view available reconfiguration options, navigate to Operating Windows Instances, select a Windows Instance, and access the Reconfigure tab.

Subscribers/Compute/WindowsInstances/Vie.title: Viewing Details of Windows Instances
Subscribers/Compute/WindowsInstances/Vie.description: To view the details of Windows Instances, navigate to Operating Windows Instances, select a Windows Instance and access the Overview tab.

Subscribers/Compute/WindowsInstances/Vol.description: To view the disks attached to this instance, navigate to Windows Instances Screen, select a Windows Instance, and access the Volumes tab.

Subscribers/Compute/WindowsInstances/Win.description: To view all available Instance operations, navigate to the Windows Instances Screen, select a Windows Instance, and access the Operations tab.

Subscribers/Compute/WindowsInstances/Wor.description: To view all the Restore Points taken for the Instance, navigate to Windows Instances Screen, select a Windows Instance, and access the Restore Points tab.

Subscribers/GettingStartedonYntraaCloud.title: Getting Started
Subscribers/GettingStartedonYntraaCloud.description: After completing the sign-up process, you receive an email with the details to access the Cloud Console.

Subscribers/intro.title: Introduction
Subscribers/intro.description: Yntraa Cloud is a powerful, all-in-one solution designed to make managing your cloud and network services simple and efficient. Whether you are a business, an organization, or an individual, Yntraa provides everything you need to keep your digital infrastructure running smoothly.

Subscribers/ManagedSecurity/DDosProtecti.title: DDoS Protection
Subscribers/ManagedSecurity/DDosProtecti.description: Distributed Denial-of-Service (DDoS) attack is a cyberattack that disrupts a website, server, or network by overwhelming it with excessive traffic from multiple compromised systems. This makes services unavailable to legitimate users and can severely impact business operations.

Subscribers/ManagedSecurity/PrivilegedAc.title: Privileged Access Management
Subscribers/ManagedSecurity/PrivilegedAc.description: In modern IT environments, Privileged Access Management (PAM) protects critical systems and sensitive data by securely managing and controlling privileged accounts. While PAM safeguards privileged credentials and enforces strict access controls, it also monitors privileged sessions and activities to prevent misuse or unauthorized access, ensuring improved security, visibility, and control across on-premises and cloud environments.

Subscribers/ManagedSecurity/YottaSmartCS.title: Yotta Smart CSOC
Subscribers/ManagedSecurity/YottaSmartCS.description: Yotta Smart Complementary Subservice Organization Controls (CSOC) is a security operations service designed to help organizations effectively manage and respond to growing and complex cyber threats. It combines advanced monitoring and automated response capabilities with expert support to detect, investigate, and remediate security incidents quickly.

Subscribers/ManagedSolutions/BackupandRe.title: Backup and Recovery
Subscribers/ManagedSolutions/BackupandRe.description: Backup and Recovery is a managed service that protects critical business data by creating secure backups and enabling fast restoration. It provides resilience against data loss, cyber threats, and system failures by maintaining reliable recovery points and reducing downtime.

Subscribers/ManagedSolutions/BareMetal.title: Bare Metal
Subscribers/ManagedSolutions/BareMetal.description: Bare Metal Service provides direct access to dedicated physical servers without virtualization overhead, ensuring optimal performance, low latency, and predictable throughput for performance-critical workloads.

Subscribers/ManagedSolutions/DevOpsasaSe.title: DevOps as a Service
Subscribers/ManagedSolutions/DevOpsasaSe.description: To activate the desired DevOps service, perform the following steps:

Subscribers/ManagedSolutions/LoadTesting.title: Load Testing as a Service
Subscribers/ManagedSolutions/LoadTesting.description: In cloud services, Load Testing as a Service (LTaaS) helps organizations evaluate the performance, scalability, and stability of applications by simulating real-world user traffic and workloads. It enables teams to identify performance bottlenecks, measure system behavior under different load conditions, and ensure that applications can handle peak demand efficiently, thereby improving reliability, user experience, and overall application performance in cloud environments.

Subscribers/ManagedSolutions/ManagedData.title: Managed Database Services
Subscribers/ManagedSolutions/ManagedData.description: Managed Databases is a cloud-based service that takes care of database setup, maintenance, scaling, and security, so organizations do not have to manage it themselves. It supports both relational and non-relational databases, ensuring high performance, availability, and reliability.

Subscribers/ManagedSolutions/MessagingSe.title: Messaging Services
Subscribers/ManagedSolutions/MessagingSe.description: In cloud services, Messaging Service enables organisations to build event-driven and real-time applications using managed messaging platforms like RabbitMQ and Kafka. It provides reliable queues and event streams without the complexity of managing infrastructure, ensuring high availability, security, and performance while teams focus on application development.

Subscribers/ManagedSolutions/ResiliencyA.title: Resiliency Assurance Service
Subscribers/ManagedSolutions/ResiliencyA.description: Resiliency Assurance Service is a disaster recovery, focused solution designed to help organizations quickly restore critical infrastructure, applications, and data after disruptions.

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/DNSManage.title: About DNS Management
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/DNSManage.description: Yntraa Cloud servers can be used as authoritative name server(s) for managing DNS records. If the Service is enabled, Cloud Console users can bring their own domains (BYOD) to use with the workloads created using Yntraa-powered clouds.

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/DNSManage.title: Linking Domains to Apiculus Cloud Console
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/DNSManage.description: The Apiculus-powered cloud has nameservers if the service provider has enabled this service. These name servers are simply fully-qualified domain names (FQDN) for the servers that store all the domain/zone information. Typically, these name servers will be in the following format:

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/DNSManage.title: Linking Domains to Yntraa Cloud
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/DNSManage.description: The Yntraa-powered cloud has nameservers if the service provider has enabled this service. These name servers are simply fully-qualified domain names (FQDN) for the servers that store all the domain/zone information. Typically, these name servers will be in the following format:

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/DNSManage.title: Managing DNS Records
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/DNSManage.description: DNS records and subdomains can be managed on Yntraa Cloud to be used with Instances, IPv4 Addresses and other workloads created on Yntraa-powered clouds. This only requires a domain to be linked with the Apiculus nameservers.

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/DNSManage.title: Switching to Another DNS Provider
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/DNSManage.description: In case you don't want to use the DNS Management Service on your Yntraa-powered cloud, you can always switch back to another provider of choice. To do so, head to your domain name provider's control panel and update the nameserver details to those of the DNS provider you wish to use.

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/IPv4-base.title: About IPv4-based Networking
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/IPv4-base.description: IPv4 Addresses form the backbone of networking operations on Yntraa. Internally, IPv4 Addresses are treated as a monetisable Service on Yntraa. However, the use of IPv4 Addresses is implicit as part of other essential Services.

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/IPv4-base.title: Establishing Site-to-Site VPN Between Two VPCs
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/IPv4-base.description: A Site-to-Site Virtual Private Network (VPN) provides a secure and encrypted connection between two Virtual Private Clouds (VPC), enabling seamless communication across geographically separated networks. This setup is commonly used to connect different environments (such as production and development) or extend on-premises infrastructure to the cloud.

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/IPv4-base.title: Managing VPN Gateways and Site-to-Site VPN
Using Remote Access VPN with VPC
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/IPv4-base.description: To use a VPN client to connect to your VPC, follow these steps:

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/L2 Networ.title: About L2 Networks
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/L2 Networ.description: L2 networks, often called data link layer networks, play a crucial role in computer networking by establishing direct connections between devices in a local network and ensuring data packets are delivered accurately to their intended destination.

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/L2 Networ.title: Adding New L2 Network
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/L2 Networ.description: To add a new L2 network, click the + New Network button. The following screen appears:

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/LoadBalan.title: About Load Balancer Instances
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/LoadBalan.description: The Yntraa Load Balancer Instances (LBI) Service allows Cloud Console users to create load balancer appliances, which are Linux Instances pre-loaded with a Citrix NetScaler VPX image. LBI can be used with VPC networks to achieve various traffic management features that are not possible using the standard VPC-based load balancing.

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/LoadBalan.title: Activating the NetScaler VPX Control Panel
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/LoadBalan.description: The following steps can be used for activating the NetScaler VPX control panel and accessing it after activation:

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/LoadBalan.title: Creating a Load Balancer Instance
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/LoadBalan.description: Load Balancer Instances can be created inside a VPC by following these simple steps:

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/NATGatewa.title: About NAT Gateways
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/NATGatewa.description: A Network Address Translation (NAT) gateway is a networking component in cloud and on-premises environments that enables resources within a private subnet (such as virtual machines or containers) to connect to the internet or other external networks, without exposing these resources directly to incoming connections from those external networks. This helps in enhancing security in cloud infrastructure by keeping resources in private subnets isolated from direct public access.

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/NATGatewa.title: Instances
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/NATGatewa.description: This section lists all the instances that are a part of this Virtual Firewall.

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/NATGatewa.title: IPv4 Addresses
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/NATGatewa.description: Each virtual gateway comes with a default IPv4 address that belongs to the Virtual Gateway governing the network. The default (source - NAT) IPv4 address can be used to access the Virtual Gateway publicly or via Remote Access (RA) VPN.

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/NATGatewa.title: Operations
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/NATGatewa.description: You can perform the following operations in the Operations section:

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/NATGatewa.title: Overview
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/NATGatewa.description: Navigate to the Overview tab to view the following details:

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/NATGatewa.title: Viewing and Managing NAT Gateways
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/NATGatewa.description: To manage a NAT gateway, follow these steps:

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/NATGatewa.title: Port Forwarding for VM via VNF
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/NATGatewa.description: This section outlines the steps to configure port forwarding for a Virtual Machine (VM) using a Virtual Network Function (VNF) in a cloud environment. Port forwarding allows external clients to securely access services (for example SSH, HTTP) hosted on the VM by mapping ports from the VNF’s public IP to the VM’s private IP. This approach improves network segmentation and control by routing all incoming traffic through the VNF layer.

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/NATGatewa.title: Port Forwarding with Public IP
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/NATGatewa.description: In Yntraa Cloud, an additional Public IP enables you to expose internal services hosted on virtual machines (VMs) to the internet by creating dedicated port forwarding rules. This approach is especially useful when multiple external IPs are needed for services such as SSH, HTTP, or custom applications. By assigning specific public IPs to particular services or VMs, you can achieve greater control, flexibility, and network segmentation.

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualFi.title: Accessing the VFI Control Panel
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualFi.description: The Virtual Firewall Service is delivered as an integration using OS images pre-loaded with pfSense or FortiGate VM.

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualFi.title: Adding Instances to a VFI-based Network
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualFi.description: Instances can be created and placed in the L2-LAN (Free Networks) created by the Virtual Firewall. These Instances use special Linux OS images, which are available as a separate OS Collection. Users can create Linux Instances, and choose the appropriate options/controls to create an Instance in the Virtual Firewall network.

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualFi.title: Creating a Virtual Firewall
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualFi.description: To create a Virtual Firewall, follow these steps:

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualFi.title: Deploying VNF and VM Within VNF
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualFi.description: This section provides a step-by-step guide for deploying a Virtual Network Function (VNF)—specifically a virtual firewall—and provisioning a Virtual Machine (VM) behind it using the Yntraa Cloud. This setup allows users to route VM traffic through the VNF, enabling secure and segmented network environments.

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualFi.title: About Firewall Instances
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualFi.description: Virtual Firewalls enables you to use Virtual Firewalls in a Virtualized Network Function (VNF) style, which include support for multiple VLANs and Public IPv4 addresses and automated service activation. This allows for more robust firewall management capabilities.

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualFi.title: Configuring Alerts
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualFi.description: To view the configured alerts or configure new ones, navigate to the Virtual Firewall and access the Alerts tab.

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualFi.title: Networking Management
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualFi.description: To view the networks that of particular Instance, navigate to the Virtual Firewall and access the Network and Security tab.

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualFi.title: Operations
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualFi.description: To view all available Instance operations, navigate to the Network and security, select a Virtual Firewall and access the Operations tab.

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualFi.title: Overview
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualFi.description: To view the below details, navigate to the Firewall Instance, select the Virtual Firewall and access the Overview tab.

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualFi.title: Reconfiguring Virtual Firewall
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualFi.description: To reconfigure the existing virtual firewall pack, navigate to the Network and Security section and select a Virtual Firewall and access the Reconfigure tab.

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualFi.title: Restore Points
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualFi.description: To view all the restore points taken for Instance, navigate to the Network and Security, select a Virtual Firewall and access the Restore Points tab.

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualFi.title: Viewing Graphs and Utilisation
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualFi.description: To view the available graphs and monitor the instance in real-time, navigate to the Virtual Firewall and access the Graphs tab.

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualFi.title: Viewing Virtual Firewall Details
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualFi.description: As the virtual firewalls are created, Cloud Console users can view the enabled Virtual Firewalls in the Network and Security > Virtual Firewalls section. The following details are shown:

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualFi.title: Volume Management
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualFi.description: To view the disks attached to this Instance, navigate to the Network and Security, select a Virtual Firewall and access the Volumes tab.

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualPr.title: About Virtual Private Clouds
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualPr.description: Details about Virtual Private Cloud operations and actions can be found in their respective sections.

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualPr.title: Creating VPC Subnets and Tiers
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualPr.description: VPCs follow the convention of 3-tiered architectures, with web, app, and DB tiers forming the norm. You can, however, configure these tiers to suit your application architecture or just follow the common convention.

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualPr.title: IPv4 Addresses and VPC
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualPr.description: IPv4 Addresses are an integral part of using VPC networking, and need to be used to access various components of the VPC. By default, a public IPv4 Address is assigned to the VR which can communicate through the internet to transmit traffic to/from the VR. This IPv4 can also be used for configuring remote access (L2TP) and site-to-site (IPSec) VPN connections.

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualPr.title: Managing Access Control on VPC Subnets
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualPr.description: Access control policies can be created using Access Control Lists (ACL) and configuring rules within these ACL (called ACL Rules). An ACL can then be applied to any tier within the VPC. These policies govern what traffic is allowed or restricted into and from your VPC network tiers.

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualPr.title: Overview
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualPr.description: To view a list of sections and the various operations or actions you can perform by going inside the particular section, click on the VPC name. Below the VPC name is an informational view where you can find the details below.

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualPr.title: Reconfiguring a VPC
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualPr.description: The Reconfigure section/tab lists your current subscription details and allows you to reconfigure the networking pack.

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualPr.title: VPC Management and Basic Operations
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualPr.description: VPC management offers the following operations. These are basic VPC management actions and don't have any impact on the actual network configurations.

Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualPr.title: Difference Between L2 Networks and VPC
Working with VPN Connections in a VPC
Subscribers/NetworkandSecurity/VirtualPr.description: The VPC feature on Yntraa provides advanced networking capabilities for use with VPN gateways and connections over an IPSec tunnel. Yntraa gives the ability to create virtual private networks (VPN) to access virtual machines inside a VPC.

Subscribers/OtherServices/Containers.title: Containers
Subscribers/OtherServices/Containers.description: To activate the desired Container Registry service, perform the following steps:

Subscribers/OtherServices/FileStorage.title: File Storage
Subscribers/OtherServices/FileStorage.description: File Storage organizes data in a hierarchical structure similar to traditional network drives, enabling easy file sharing and collaboration. It is ideal for document storage, shared workspaces, and everyday file access across users and applications.

Subscribers/SigningIn.title: Signing In
Subscribers/SigningIn.description: To Sign In to your Yntraa Cloud, follow these steps:

Subscribers/SigningUp.title: Signing Up
Subscribers/SigningUp.description: To get started with the Yntraa Cloud, you must create an account on the One Yotta account.

Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/AboutBl.title: About Block Volumes
Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/AboutBl.description: Yntraa block volumes are the underlying software-defined volume management layer that powers various storage-related or storage-driven cloud services. Block Volumes can be used with Linux and Windows instances as root and data disks, with Kubernetes as persistent storage, or simply as a storehouse for snapshots and images.

Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Creatin.title: Creating Data Disk
Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Creatin.description: To create a Block Volume data disk, follow the below steps:

Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Details.title: Operations
Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Details.description: To view all available Block Volume operations, navigate to the Storage, select the Block Volumes and access the Operations tab. The following window appears:

Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Details.title: Overview
Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Details.description: Navigate to the Overview tab to view the following details:

Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Details.title: Resize the Block Volume
Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Details.description: To resize an available Block Volumes, navigate to Storage, select the Block Volumes and access the Resize tab.

Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Details.title: Creating Automated Restore Point Schedules
Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Details.description: Yntraa Cloud users can create restore point policies for their root and data disks. These schedules enable for strategic management of data retention policies and can be used as an alternative backup solution. To create or manage the restore point schedules, navigate to the Schedules section of disk details.

Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Details.title: Working with Disk Restore Points
Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Details.description: Yntraa block volumes service provides extensive functionality for managing disk restore points. Restore points are point-in-time 'images' of a disk contents and can be used as a restoration point for the parent disk. The following sections outline all available restore points functions and capabilities in the Yntraa Cloud.

Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/UsingBl.title: Using Block Volumes with Kubernetes
Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/UsingBl.description: Yntraa Kubernetes also uses the underlying Block Volumes service to create persistent volumes for Kubernetes clusters. Yntraa Cloud users can specify the root disk size for each cluster node (control and worker), and the Block Volume service creates the nodes in the cluster with a root disk of the specified size.

Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Working.title: Working with Instance Volumes
Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Working.description: Each Instance on Yntraa Cloud (Linux or Windows) includes a 'root' disk or volume. This is the primary disk partition on the Instance, where the operating system, data, components and files reside on the root disk.

Subscribers/Storage/ObjectStorageBuckets.title: About Yntraa Cloud Object Storage
Subscribers/Storage/ObjectStorageBuckets.description: Yntraa cloud object storage provides scalable and flexible storage for unstructured data in the form of objects. In object storage, data is stored as discrete objects containing data, metadata, and a unique identifier. Yntraa Cloud delivers the service through an integration with Object Storage Platform.

Subscribers/Storage/ObjectStorageBuckets.title: Feature List and Integration Details
Subscribers/Storage/ObjectStorageBuckets.description: Yntraa Storage is delivered as an integration with a leading object storage platform. The integration functions using API-driven checkpoints.

Subscribers/Storage/ObjectStorageBuckets.title: Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
Subscribers/Storage/ObjectStorageBuckets.description: What is object storage in the context of Yntraa Cloud?object storage is a scalable and durable storage solution for managing unstructured data such as files, images, videos, and backups.

Subscribers/ToolsandUtilities/AboutTools.title: About Tools and Utilities
Subscribers/ToolsandUtilities/AboutTools.description: The Tools and Utilities sections in the Yntraa Cloud Console keep a record of cloud management utilities and assets that might be needed to use specific sections of the Yntraa powered cloud.

Subscribers/ToolsandUtilities/AddingVPNG.title: Adding VPN Gateways
Subscribers/ToolsandUtilities/AddingVPNG.description: VPN Gateways allow you to establish secure connections between networks and cloud resources. The Yntraa Cloud Console enables you to create and manage VPN Gateways to ensure safe and reliable communication using encrypted tunnels over the internet.

Subscribers/ToolsandUtilities/ManagingCu.title: Managing Custom Templates and Images
Subscribers/ToolsandUtilities/ManagingCu.description: Custom OS templates can be created from a Restore Point. To view custom templates, navigate to Tools and Utilities > My Images. The following details are displayed:

Subscribers/ToolsandUtilities/ManagingIn.title: Managing Instance and Disk Restore Points
Subscribers/ToolsandUtilities/ManagingIn.description: Restore points use the Yntraa Block Volumes service and occupy billable storage space.

Subscribers/ToolsandUtilities/ManagingSS.title: Managing SSH Keys and Key Pairs for Linux Instances
Subscribers/ToolsandUtilities/ManagingSS.description: The Yntraa Cloud Console allows you to create secure shell connections to Instances in your account by using SSH keys, in addition to using Instance passwords. Since passwords can be easily compromised, SSH keys provide a more secure alternative. These keys function as encrypted signatures and work only when their public and private components match.
